fellow.name: Allen Weber
fellow.syncTarget: Logged in hours workbook X07 and mirrored to the cohort team wiki

project.title: Small-City Zoning Reform in New England

week.summary: Set up the project repository and pulled the first parcel records for both cities.
week.task: Pull Worcester parcel and permit records

week.summary: Finished the New Haven pull and began the geocoding step that maps permits to corridors.
week.task: Pull New Haven parcel and permit records
week.blocker: Two New Haven corridors have sparse 2019 records and need hand review

week.summary: Permit pull complete for both cities. The displacement index now runs end to end on a small test set.
week.task: Join American Community Survey tenure and rent burden estimates
First pass of the three-component index on a test corridor set
week.blocker: None blocking, two open questions held for the Tuesday check-in

week.summary: Index scored across all corridors. Ordering matches expectations: upzoned without protection scores highest.
week.task: Run index on the full corridor panel
Draft the mid-summer memo for the mentor

observations.item: Upzoned corridors without protections show the highest index values, comparison corridors the lowest.
Permit timing lags zoning changes unevenly across the two cities, as expected.
Turnover is the noisiest of the three components and may be down-weighted later.
